rulings.Case Title: GARRITT BLANZ ET AL VS. ROBERT C HINCKLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement On Record [Ccp 664.6]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 3, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANTS ROBERT HINCKLEY AND CHRISTINE BARTLETT HINCKLEY'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO TERMS OF SETTLEMENT ON RECORD [CCP 664.6] is GRANTED. Parties to prepare an order for the Court to execute regarding the Release Agreement repeating verbatim the language regarding the release as stated on the record before judge Bradstreet. Proposed order to be submitted no later than 9:00 a.m. on March 3, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DAMIEN QUINN VS. BELLROSE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On Verified Fifth Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 3, 2022 line 3. CROSS-DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON VERIFIED FIFTH AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is DENIED. Cross-Defendant's Objections to Cross-Complainant's evidence are overruled, except objection number 12, which is sustained on hearsay grounds. Motion is DENIED as to Belrose, LLC. Motion for summary judgment is framed by the pleadings. Moving party fails to show that the operative complaint asserts any claims at issue in this motion by Belrose, LLC against the moving party. No judgment may be entered on claims not asserted in the complaint. Prevailing party to prepare an order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). Motion is DENIED as to Kaufman. Triable issues of fact exist as to knowledge, breach of duty and good faith. Prevailing party to prepare an order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
